title: Lean Startup Practice Readiness

introduction.subtitle: Maturity assessment. 7-9 min.
introduction.description:
Lean Startup Practice Readiness helps you assess Problem Hypothesis, MVP Learning Design, Actionable Metrics, Pivot Discipline through concrete operating questions. Answer from observable behaviour and current evidence, not aspiration.

This educational quiz runs entirely in your browser. You receive the baseline result without account creation or email.
introduction.privacyNote: Your answers stay in this browser. The result belongs to you. Sharing is explicit and share-safe.
introduction.disclaimer: Educational diagnostic only. Not legal, tax, financial, investment, medical, employment, regulatory, or psychometric certification advice.

questions.text: How clearly is the riskiest customer problem hypothesis stated before building?

options.text: A. No concrete example shows the riskiest customer problem hypothesis; the answer relies on intention, memory, or individual judgement.
B. There are isolated examples of the riskiest customer problem hypothesis, but they are inconsistent, hard to verify, or depend on one person.
C. Recent examples show the riskiest customer problem hypothesis in practice, with enough detail to judge quality and repeatability.
D. The riskiest customer problem hypothesis is repeatedly demonstrated under pressure and changes decisions, ownership, or trade-offs.
Not sure / not applicable.

conceptHelp.title: Problem Hypothesis: How clearly is the riskiest customer problem hypothesis stated before building
conceptHelp.summary: Problem Hypothesis is tested here through a concrete operating question: "How clearly is the riskiest customer problem hypothesis stated before building?". Use the answer choices to separate aspiration from current practice.
conceptHelp.whyItMatters: Problem Hypothesis matters because weak practice in this area can make the whole result look more mature than the operating system really is.
conceptHelp.beforeAnswering: Use recent observable behaviour: artifacts, decisions, customer evidence, owners, reviews, or shipped work. Do not score intention as practice.
conceptHelp.example: A strong answer would show current evidence, a named owner, and one recent decision changed by this practice.
